reason: Correcting permissions on /etc/shadow to restrict access is a standard STIG hardening procedure. The recommended command removes all permissions for all users, which is the standard way to ensure only the root user (via specific processes) can access sensitive password hashes. This does not break system functionality as long as the file remains owned by root.
estimated_impact: authentication/authorization affected; Incorrectly applying permissions could theoretically impact specific legacy services that read shadow directly, though this is rare on modern RHEL 9 systems.
